description: Adversary gains initial foothold via spearphishing or drive-by compromise
common_indicators: Suspicious emails, malicious attachments, credential harvesting sites, exploit kits

description: Adversary executes malicious code on victim system
common_indicators: PowerShell execution, command-line activity, script execution, suspicious process creation

description: Adversary maintains foothold across reboots and credential changes
common_indicators: New services, scheduled tasks, registry run keys, startup folder modifications, backdoors
UAC bypass, kernel exploits, sudo abuse, token manipulation, DLL hijacking

description: Adversary avoids detection by security controls
common_indicators: Process injection, file deletion, log clearing, rootkits, AV disabling, masquerading
LSASS dumping, password spraying, keylogging, credential files access, hash cracking

description: Adversary explores environment and gathers information
common_indicators: Network scanning, user enumeration, file/directory discovery, system info queries
RDP usage, PSExec, WMI, pass-the-hash, stolen credentials, SMB connections
File staging, clipboard data, screen capture, audio/video capture, email collection
Beaconing to external IPs, DNS tunneling, encrypted channels, web protocols abuse

description: Adversary steals data from the environment
common_indicators: Large outbound transfers, unusual protocols, data compression, cloud storage uploads

description: Adversary disrupts availability or integrity of systems/data
common_indicators: Ransomware encryption, data destruction, service stop, defacement, DoS
